text: Students are expected to attend all scheduled classes, laboratories, and seminars. A minimum of 75% attendance is required in each course to be eligible to sit for the final examinations. Excused absences due to illness or official university events must be documented and submitted to the Dean's office within 5 business days.

Course grades are awarded on a scale from A to F. The minimum passing grade for any undergraduate course is a D (1.0 grade point, equivalent to 50-59%). A grade of F (0.0 grade points) denotes failure. GPA is calculated on a 4.0 scale.

Academic honesty is fundamental to the university community. Plagiarism, cheating, collusion, or any form of misrepresentation in academic work will result in disciplinary action. First offenses typically result in a failing grade for the assignment or course; repeat offenses will lead to suspension or expulsion.

Students may add or drop courses without academic penalty during the first two weeks of the semester (the Add/Drop Period). Withdrawals after this period and before the tenth week will receive a grade of 'W' on the transcript. Late withdrawals are not permitted except for documented medical or personal emergencies.

Undergraduate students who achieve a semester grade point average (GPA) of 3.50 or higher while completing a minimum of 12 letter-graded credits, with no grades below C, are eligible for the Dean's List honors for that semester.
